description: Initial intervention ontology for gnoma — curated aging-relevant molecular interventions

interventions.name: Rapamycin (mTOR inhibition)
interventions.mechanism: Allosteric inhibition of mTORC1 via FKBP12 binding, reducing protein synthesis and promoting autophagy
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in multiple model organisms; reduces senescence markers; improves immune function in elderly
interventions.notes: Most robust pharmacological lifespan extension in mammals

interventions.name: Metformin (AMPK activation)
interventions.mechanism: Activates AMPK via mitochondrial complex I inhibition; downstream mTOR suppression
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduced all-cause mortality in diabetic patients; TAME trial ongoing for aging
interventions.notes: FDA-approved; TAME trial is first anti-aging clinical trial

interventions.name: NMN supplementation (NAD+ precursor)
interventions.mechanism: Nicotinamide mononucleotide converted to NAD+ by NMNAT enzymes; restores age-declined NAD+ levels
interventions.known_aging_effect: Improves mitochondrial function, insulin sensitivity, and vascular health in aged mice; human trials showing modest NAD+ elevation

interventions.name: NR supplementation (NAD+ precursor)
interventions.mechanism: Nicotinamide riboside phosphorylated by NRK1/2 to NMN then to NAD+
interventions.known_aging_effect: Improves stem cell function and lifespan in aged mice; elevates blood NAD+ in humans

interventions.name: Dasatinib + Quercetin (senolytic)
interventions.mechanism: Dasatinib inhibits tyrosine kinases in senescent cell survival networks; quercetin inhibits BCL-2/BCL-XL anti-apoptotic pathways
interventions.known_aging_effect: Clears senescent cells; improves physical function and reduces frailty in aged mice; first human senolytic trial in IPF
interventions.notes: First-generation senolytic combination; Dasatinib is FDA-approved for CML

interventions.name: Navitoclax / ABT-263 (senolytic)
interventions.mechanism: BH3 mimetic inhibiting BCL-2, BCL-XL, BCL-W; selectively induces apoptosis in senescent cells
interventions.known_aging_effect: Clears senescent hematopoietic stem cells; rejuvenates aged HSC compartment in mice
interventions.notes: Thrombocytopenia side effect limits clinical use

interventions.name: OSKM partial reprogramming (cyclic)
interventions.mechanism: Transient expression of Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, c-Myc resets epigenetic age without full dedifferentiation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reverses epigenetic clocks; improves tissue function in progeroid and naturally aged mice
interventions.notes: c-Myc is a proto-oncogene; continuous expression causes teratomas. Cyclic/pulsed protocols mitigate risk.

interventions.name: OSK partial reprogramming (Myc-free)
interventions.mechanism: Oct4, Sox2, Klf4 without c-Myc; epigenetic rejuvenation with lower oncogenic risk
interventions.known_aging_effect: Restores youthful gene expression and epigenetic marks in aged mouse retinal ganglion cells; restores vision
interventions.notes: Lu et al. 2020 — landmark study showing vision restoration in aged mice via OSK

interventions.name: Resveratrol (SIRT1 activation)
interventions.mechanism: Polyphenol activating SIRT1 deacetylase; mimics caloric restriction signaling
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in yeast and short-lived fish; improves metabolic health in obese mice; mixed human results
interventions.notes: Bioavailability issues; direct SIRT1 activation mechanism debated

interventions.name: Spermidine supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Natural polyamine that induces autophagy via hypusination of eIF5A and TFEB activation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in yeast, flies, worms, mice; reduces cardiac aging; epidemiological association with reduced mortality

interventions.name: Senolytics — Fisetin
interventions.mechanism: Flavonoid with senolytic and senomorphic properties; inhibits multiple kinases in senescent cell survival pathways
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces senescence markers and extends healthspan in aged mice; human trials ongoing
interventions.notes: Better tolerated than navitoclax; ongoing AFFIRM trial

interventions.name: SIRT6 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: SIRT6 deacetylase promotes DNA repair, suppresses NF-κB, maintains telomere integrity
interventions.known_aging_effect: Male mice with SIRT6 OE live ~15% longer; improves genomic stability

interventions.name: Telomerase (TERT) overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Catalytic subunit of telomerase; extends telomeres and delays replicative senescence
interventions.known_aging_effect: AAV-TERT extends healthspan in aged mice without increased cancer; reverses pulmonary fibrosis markers
interventions.notes: Oncogenic risk depends on context; non-canonical TERT functions (mitochondrial) may contribute

interventions.name: p16INK4a knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces p16-mediated cell cycle arrest; allows senescent cells to re-enter cycle
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces senescence burden; but loss of tumor suppression is a major risk
interventions.notes: p16 is a major tumor suppressor. KD increases cancer risk substantially. Included for completeness but safety filter should block.

interventions.name: GDF11 supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Circulating factor identified in parabiosis experiments; may promote neurogenesis and muscle repair
interventions.known_aging_effect: Controversial — initial claims of cardiac and neural rejuvenation in heterochronic parabiosis; subsequent studies found confounding with GDF8
interventions.notes: Highly debated in the field; include for model testing but low confidence

interventions.name: Alpha-ketoglutarate (αKG) supplementation
interventions.mechanism: TCA cycle intermediate; cofactor for TET demethylases and JmjC histone demethylases; modulates epigenetic landscape
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in C. elegans by ~50%; reduces frailty in aged mice; reduces biological age in humans (TruAge pilot)

interventions.name: HDAC inhibitor — Vorinostat (SAHA)
interventions.mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibitor; increases histone acetylation, opens chromatin, reactivates silenced genes
interventions.known_aging_effect: Modulates senescence programs; FDA-approved for CTCL; impacts on aging-specific epigenetic marks under investigation
interventions.notes: FDA-approved; epigenetic aging effects are context-dependent

interventions.name: DNMT inhibitor — 5-Azacytidine (decitabine)
interventions.mechanism: Nucleoside analog incorporated into DNA; traps DNMT1 and causes passive demethylation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reverses age-associated hypermethylation; FDA-approved for MDS; low-dose protocols under investigation for epigenetic rejuvenation
interventions.notes: Dose-dependent toxicity; low-dose protocols may be viable for rejuvenation

interventions.name: Klotho overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Anti-aging hormone; inhibits insulin/IGF-1 signaling, suppresses Wnt signaling, reduces oxidative stress
interventions.known_aging_effect: Klotho-deficient mice show premature aging; OE extends lifespan by ~30%; improves cognition in aged mice

interventions.name: FOXO3 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Forkhead transcription factor; activates stress resistance, autophagy, and DNA repair gene programs
interventions.known_aging_effect: FOXO3 SNPs are the most replicated human longevity-associated variants; OE extends lifespan in model organisms
interventions.notes: Strong human genetics evidence for longevity association

interventions.name: p53 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces p53-mediated cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in response to DNA damage
interventions.known_aging_effect: Delays senescence onset but massively increases cancer susceptibility
interventions.notes: Included as a negative control — safety filter must block this

interventions.name: MYC overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Master transcription factor driving proliferation, metabolism, and biosynthesis
interventions.known_aging_effect: Component of OSKM but drives tumorigenesis independently
interventions.notes: Known oncogene — included as negative control for safety filter validation

interventions.name: TFAM overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Mitochondrial transcription factor A; drives mitochondrial DNA replication and transcription
interventions.known_aging_effect: Rescues age-associated mitochondrial dysfunction in mouse models

interventions.name: NRF2 activation (sulforaphane)
interventions.mechanism: Sulforaphane inhibits KEAP1, stabilizing NRF2; activates ARE-mediated antioxidant gene expression
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces oxidative damage markers; improves proteostasis; modest lifespan extension in some models

interventions.name: Caloric restriction mimetic — 2-deoxyglucose
interventions.mechanism: Glycolysis inhibitor; mimics glucose deprivation, activating AMPK and autophagy pathways
interventions.known_aging_effect: Mimics some caloric restriction transcriptomic signatures; mixed lifespan results
interventions.notes: Toxicity at high doses limits in vivo use

interventions.name: IL6 knockdown (SASP suppression)
interventions.mechanism: Reduces interleukin-6 secretion from senescent cells; attenuates SASP-driven chronic inflammation and paracrine senescence spreading
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduced systemic IL-6 correlates with healthspan improvement; IL-6 blockade reduces frailty markers in aged mice
interventions.notes: IL-6 has pleiotropic roles; immune suppression risk; anti-IL-6 antibody tocilizumab is FDA-approved

interventions.name: IL1B knockdown (SASP suppression)
interventions.mechanism: Suppresses interleukin-1β, a master regulator of SASP; reduces downstream NF-κB activation and cytokine cascades
interventions.known_aging_effect: IL-1β drives age-related inflammation; genetic reduction improves metabolic health and reduces inflammaging markers
interventions.notes: Anakinra (IL-1R antagonist) and canakinumab (anti-IL-1β antibody) are FDA-approved and studied in aging contexts

interventions.name: CXCL8 knockdown (SASP suppression)
interventions.mechanism: Reduces IL-8/CXCL8 chemokine secretion from senescent cells; limits neutrophil recruitment and tissue inflammation
interventions.known_aging_effect: CXCL8 is a core SASP component; reduction decreases immune-mediated tissue damage in aging
interventions.notes: CXCL8 has no mouse ortholog (IL-8); CXCR1/2 receptor antagonists are available as proxies

interventions.name: TNF knockdown (SASP suppression)
interventions.mechanism: Reduces tumor necrosis factor alpha; attenuates NF-κB-mediated inflammatory gene expression and paracrine senescence
interventions.known_aging_effect: TNF-α increases with age; anti-TNF therapy improves physical function and reduces frailty in aged mice
interventions.notes: Anti-TNF biologics (etanercept, adalimumab) are FDA-approved and show benefits in aging-related inflammation

interventions.name: NFKB1 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces NF-κB p50 subunit activity; suppresses transcription of SASP cytokines, pro-inflammatory mediators, and anti-apoptotic genes
interventions.known_aging_effect: NF-κB is master regulator of inflammaging; tissue-specific NF-κB inhibition extends healthspan in mice
interventions.notes: NF-κB also has tumor suppressor roles in certain contexts; complete blockade risks immunosuppression

interventions.name: RPTOR knockdown (mTORC1 inhibition)
interventions.mechanism: Knockdown of Raptor, the essential mTORC1 scaffold; selectively inhibits mTORC1 without affecting mTORC2
interventions.known_aging_effect: Selective mTORC1 inhibition extends lifespan in Drosophila and C. elegans; improves proteostasis and reduces senescence
interventions.notes: More selective than rapamycin for mTORC1; avoids mTORC2 feedback inhibition

interventions.name: RPS6KB1 knockdown (S6K1 inhibition)
interventions.mechanism: Knockdown of S6 kinase 1, a key mTORC1 substrate; reduces protein synthesis and anabolic signaling
interventions.known_aging_effect: S6K1 knockout female mice live ~19% longer; improves insulin sensitivity; phenocopies some rapamycin effects
interventions.notes: Selman et al. 2009 — clean genetic evidence for S6K1 in mammalian lifespan

interventions.name: RB1 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces retinoblastoma protein function; releases E2F transcription factors from repression, allowing cell cycle re-entry
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces senescence burden in theory; however, RB1 is a major tumor suppressor
interventions.notes: RB1 is a canonical tumor suppressor; knockdown carries high cancer risk. Included for completeness; safety filter should block.

interventions.name: MDM2 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: MDM2 is the primary E3 ubiquitin ligase for p53; knockdown stabilizes p53, promoting senescence and apoptosis of damaged cells
interventions.known_aging_effect: Increased p53 activity from MDM2 reduction could paradoxically promote or reduce aging depending on context and tissue
interventions.notes: Counterintuitive: MDM2 KD activates p53 (tumor suppressor). MDM2 inhibitors (nutlin-3) are in oncology trials.

interventions.name: EZH2 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces EZH2-mediated H3K27me3 deposition; derepresses Polycomb-silenced genes including pro-aging targets
interventions.known_aging_effect: EZH2 declines with age in some tissues; in others it increases aberrantly; KD may restore youthful gene expression patterns
interventions.notes: Context-dependent; EZH2 can be tumor suppressive or oncogenic depending on tissue type

interventions.name: SUV39H1 knockdown
interventions.mechanism: Reduces H3K9me3 deposition; affects constitutive heterochromatin maintenance and pericentromeric silencing
interventions.known_aging_effect: Loss of heterochromatin (H3K9me3) is a hallmark of aging; however, SUV39H1 also silences retrotransposons — reduction risks genomic instability
interventions.notes: Heterochromatin loss in aging suggests restoration may be beneficial; but retrotransposon derepression is a major risk

interventions.name: SIRT1 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Class III HDAC deacetylating H3K9, H4K16, and non-histone targets (p53, NF-κB, PGC-1α); promotes stress resistance and metabolic health
interventions.known_aging_effect: SIRT1 OE improves metabolic phenotypes in mice on high-fat diet; extends healthspan in some models; brain-specific OE protects against neurodegeneration
interventions.notes: Modest effect size compared to SIRT6 OE; physiological levels may already be close to optimal

interventions.name: SIRT3 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Mitochondrial deacetylase; activates ETC complexes, SOD2, IDH2, and LCAD; reduces mitochondrial ROS and improves ATP production
interventions.known_aging_effect: SIRT3 declines with age; OE reduces oxidative stress and improves hearing, cardiac function, and metabolic health in aged mice
interventions.notes: SIRT3 is a tumor suppressor in some contexts

interventions.name: SIRT7 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Nucleolar deacetylase; regulates rDNA transcription by RNA Pol I and maintains genomic stability; deacetylates H3K18
interventions.known_aging_effect: SIRT7-deficient mice show signs of premature aging with reduced lifespan; OE may preserve chromatin integrity with age
interventions.notes: Least studied sirtuin in aging context; evidence primarily from loss-of-function models

interventions.name: ATG5 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Essential autophagy factor forming the ATG12-ATG5 conjugate; promotes autophagosome elongation and autophagic flux
interventions.known_aging_effect: Systemic ATG5 OE extends median lifespan by ~17% in mice; improves motor function, insulin sensitivity, and reduces cancer
interventions.notes: Pyo et al. 2013 — clean mammalian lifespan extension via autophagy induction

interventions.name: BECN1 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Beclin-1 initiates autophagosome nucleation as part of the class III PI3K complex; promotes autophagic flux
interventions.known_aging_effect: Heterozygous Beclin-1 knockin mice (monoallelic expression equivalent to systemic OE) have extended lifespan and improved healthspan
interventions.notes: Beclin-1 F121A knockin allele elevates autophagy chronically with beneficial effects

interventions.name: TFEB overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Master transcription factor of CLEAR network; coordinately upregulates autophagy and lysosomal biogenesis genes
interventions.known_aging_effect: TFEB activation in aged muscle restores proteostasis; reduces protein aggregates; improves function in models of neurodegeneration
interventions.notes: Increasingly recognized as a key longevity node; mTORC1 phosphorylation inactivates TFEB

interventions.name: SOD2 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Mitochondrial manganese superoxide dismutase; converts superoxide to hydrogen peroxide, reducing mitochondrial oxidative damage
interventions.known_aging_effect: SOD2 heterozygous KO shortens lifespan; OE reduces oxidative damage but results on lifespan are mixed; improves cardiomyopathy in aged mice
interventions.notes: Results are more modest than initially anticipated; free radical theory of aging may be oversimplified

interventions.name: CAT overexpression (catalase, mitochondrial)
interventions.mechanism: Mitochondrially targeted catalase (mCAT) degrades H2O2 in the mitochondrial matrix; reduces mitochondrial oxidative damage
interventions.known_aging_effect: mCAT transgenic mice live ~20% longer; reduced cardiac aging, mitochondrial deletions, and cancer incidence
interventions.notes: Schriner et al. 2005 — landmark paper; mitochondrial targeting is critical for effect

interventions.name: GPX1 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Glutathione peroxidase 1 reduces hydrogen peroxide and lipid hydroperoxides using glutathione as electron donor
interventions.known_aging_effect: GPX1 OE provides modest protection against oxidative stress-induced damage; lifespan extension inconsistent across models
interventions.notes: Less robust than mCAT for lifespan; beneficial for specific age-related pathologies

interventions.name: NANOG overexpression
interventions.mechanism: Core pluripotency transcription factor; activates stem cell gene programs and suppresses differentiation; reverses some aging phenotypes in muscle and vascular cells
interventions.known_aging_effect: NANOG OE rejuvenates aged muscle stem cells; restores vascular smooth muscle cell function; activates IGF-1/PI3K and Wnt signaling
interventions.notes: NANOG is associated with cancer stem cells; high oncogenic risk in vivo. Cellular rejuvenation data is promising but safety is a major concern.

interventions.name: LGR5 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: LGR5 is a marker and enhancer of intestinal stem cell identity; amplifies R-spondin/Wnt signaling to maintain stem cell pools
interventions.known_aging_effect: Intestinal stem cell function declines with age; LGR5+ cell maintenance improves tissue regeneration; relevant for gut aging
interventions.notes: LGR5 is an intestinal cancer stem cell marker; Wnt pathway activation carries oncogenic risk in colon

interventions.name: PPARGC1A / PGC-1α overexpression
interventions.mechanism: PGC-1α is the master co-activator of mitochondrial biogenesis; induces mitochondrial content, oxidative capacity, and energy metabolism
interventions.known_aging_effect: PGC-1α declines with age; muscle-specific OE preserves function in aged mice; reduces age-related neurodegeneration in some models
interventions.notes: Tissue-specific expression important; hepatic OE may cause lipid mobilization complications

interventions.name: PINK1 overexpression
interventions.mechanism: PINK1 kinase accumulates on damaged mitochondria; recruits Parkin E3 ligase to ubiquitinate outer membrane proteins and trigger mitophagy
interventions.known_aging_effect: PINK1 OE enhances mitophagy and protects against age-related mitochondrial dysfunction; relevant to Parkinson's disease pathology
interventions.notes: PINK1 mutations cause early-onset Parkinson's; restoration may be neuroprotective in aging

interventions.name: UBX0101 (senolytic, MDM2/p53 axis)
interventions.mechanism: MDM2 inhibitor that stabilizes p53 in senescent cells, promoting their selective apoptosis; senescent cells have altered p53 dynamics
interventions.known_aging_effect: Clears senescent cells in articular cartilage; reduced osteoarthritis pathology in mouse models; entered Phase 1/2 clinical trials
interventions.notes: Unity Biotechnology compound; Phase 2 trial for knee OA was discontinued but mechanism validated

interventions.name: ABT-737 (senolytic BH3 mimetic)
interventions.mechanism: Second-generation BH3 mimetic inhibiting BCL-2 and BCL-XL; more potent than navitoclax for senescent cell clearance in some tissues
interventions.known_aging_effect: Clears senescent cells in vitro with high potency; improves tissue function in aged mouse models
interventions.notes: Predecessor to navitoclax; poor oral bioavailability limits in vivo use but useful for mechanistic studies

interventions.name: Everolimus (mTOR inhibitor, rapalog)
interventions.mechanism: Rapalog (rapamycin analog); allosteric mTORC1 inhibitor via FKBP12; improved pharmacokinetics and oral bioavailability vs rapamycin
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in aged mice; improves immune function in elderly humans (TORC1 inhibition rejuvenates aging immune responses in clinical trial)
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for multiple indications; Novartis clinical trial showed immune rejuvenation in elderly volunteers

interventions.name: Torin1 (mTOR kinase inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: ATP-competitive mTOR kinase inhibitor; inhibits both mTORC1 and mTORC2; more complete mTOR inhibition than rapalogs
interventions.known_aging_effect: More potent than rapamycin for autophagy induction; extends lifespan in C. elegans; being evaluated in aging models
interventions.notes: Research tool compound; not FDA-approved; mTORC2 inhibition may have undesired metabolic effects

interventions.name: AICAR (AMPK activator)
interventions.mechanism: 5-Aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide ribonucleotide; converted to ZMP (AMP analog), directly activating AMPK by mimicking energy depletion
interventions.known_aging_effect: Improves mitochondrial biogenesis and metabolic function in aged rodents; extends healthspan in some models
interventions.notes: Short half-life; parenteral administration; exercise mimetic properties; combinable with metformin but may have redundant effects

interventions.name: SRT1720 (SIRT1 activator)
interventions.mechanism: Synthetic SIRT1 activating compound (STAC); allosteric activator working through hydrophobic STAC-binding domain of SIRT1
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in obese mice on high-fat diet; improves metabolic health; some evidence for healthspan extension in standard diet
interventions.notes: More potent and bioavailable than resveratrol; mechanism initially debated, later validated by structural biology

interventions.name: SRT2104 (SIRT1 activator)
interventions.mechanism: Second-generation SIRT1 activating compound; allosteric SIRT1 activation with improved pharmacokinetics
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in aged male mice on standard diet; improves bone and muscle mass; completed Phase 1 and 2 clinical trials
interventions.notes: Mitchell et al. 2014 — lifespan extension in aged mice; human trials show safety and modest biomarker effects

interventions.name: 78c (CD38 inhibitor, NAD+ booster)
interventions.mechanism: Thiazoloquin(az)olin(on)e compound inhibiting CD38 NADase; prevents NAD+ degradation, elevating intracellular NAD+ levels
interventions.known_aging_effect: CD38 increases with age and is a primary NAD+-consuming enzyme; 78c restores youthful NAD+ levels in aged mice, improving mitochondrial function
interventions.notes: Camacho-Pereira et al. 2016 — identified CD38 as key NAD+ consumer in aging; 78c is potent inhibitor

interventions.name: Apigenin (CD38 inhibitor / NAD+ booster)
interventions.mechanism: Flavonoid that inhibits CD38 enzymatic activity; elevates NAD+ levels and activates sirtuins as secondary effect
interventions.known_aging_effect: Elevates NAD+ levels in vivo; improves mitochondrial function; anti-inflammatory properties via NF-κB inhibition
interventions.notes: Natural compound with favorable safety profile; weaker than synthetic CD38 inhibitors but more accessible

interventions.name: Ruxolitinib (JAK1/2 inhibitor / senomorphic)
interventions.mechanism: JAK1/2 inhibitor blocking cytokine receptor signaling; reduces SASP secretion and inflammaging without eliminating senescent cells
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces frailty and improves physical function in aged mice; phase 2 trial showed reduced inflammatory markers in elderly
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for myelofibrosis and alopecia areata; senomorphic (SASP suppressor) rather than senolytic mechanism

interventions.name: Anakinra (IL-1R antagonist / anti-inflammaging)
interventions.mechanism: Recombinant human IL-1 receptor antagonist; blocks IL-1α and IL-1β signaling, suppressing downstream inflammatory cascades
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces SASP-driven inflammation; improved frailty and metabolic markers in older adults in observational studies
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for rheumatoid arthritis; daily subcutaneous injection limits convenience; IL-18 not blocked

interventions.name: MitoQ (mitochondria-targeted antioxidant)
interventions.mechanism: Mitoquinone; ubiquinol antioxidant covalently linked to triphenylphosphonium; accumulates ~1000-fold in mitochondria; scavenges superoxide and lipid peroxyl radicals
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces age-related mitochondrial dysfunction and oxidative damage; improves vascular function in aged mice and in a human clinical trial
interventions.notes: Oral bioavailability; mitochondrial targeting is key advantage over conventional antioxidants

interventions.name: SS-31 / Elamipretide (mitochondria-targeted peptide)
interventions.mechanism: Szeto-Schiller peptide targeting cardiolipin on the inner mitochondrial membrane; stabilizes cristae structure, reduces ROS, and improves ETC efficiency
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reverses multiple aging hallmarks in aged mice; improves kidney, skeletal muscle, and cardiac function; reduces mitochondrial dysfunction
interventions.notes: Subcutaneous administration; clinical trials ongoing for heart failure and kidney disease

interventions.name: Tazemetostat (EZH2 inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: SAM-competitive inhibitor of EZH2 methyltransferase; reduces H3K27me3 and derepresses polycomb target genes
interventions.known_aging_effect: Modulates aging-associated epigenetic drift; restores expression of Polycomb-silenced genes; being evaluated for rejuvenation potential
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for epithelioid sarcoma; well-characterized pharmacology; aging applications are exploratory

interventions.name: JQ1 (BET bromodomain inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: Competitive inhibitor of BET bromodomain proteins (BRD2/3/4/T); displaces from acetylated histones; suppresses MYC transcription and inflammatory gene programs
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces SASP gene expression; anti-inflammatory effects in senescent cells; extends healthspan in some Drosophila models
interventions.notes: Research tool compound; clinical BET inhibitors (OTX015, birabresib) in development; potential for senomorphic applications

interventions.name: Chaetocin (SUV39H1 inhibitor / H3K9me3 modulator)
interventions.mechanism: Natural product inhibitor of SUV39H1 histone methyltransferase; reduces H3K9me3 and affects heterochromatin maintenance
interventions.known_aging_effect: Modulates constitutive heterochromatin; extends Drosophila lifespan in some contexts; complex effects on aging-associated chromatin changes
interventions.notes: Research compound with toxicity concerns; heterochromatin modulation in aging is an active area but tools are limited

interventions.name: Vitamin C (TET activator / ascorbic acid)
interventions.mechanism: Ascorbic acid is an essential cofactor for TET dioxygenases; reduces Fe(III) to Fe(II) to maintain TET enzymatic activity and promote DNA demethylation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Restores TET activity; reverses aberrant hypermethylation; synergizes with αKG to promote active DNA demethylation and rejuvenation
interventions.notes: Widely used supplement; high-dose IV vitamin C has distinct pharmacokinetics from oral supplementation

interventions.name: OCT4 alone (partial reprogramming)
interventions.mechanism: Pioneer transcription factor that opens closed chromatin; alone can partially reset epigenetic age in somatic cells without full dedifferentiation
interventions.known_aging_effect: OCT4 alone can partially rejuvenate aged cells epigenetically; lower risk profile than full OSKM
interventions.notes: Pioneer factor activity makes OCT4 uniquely capable of partial reprogramming; less risk than Myc-containing cocktails

interventions.name: SOX2 alone (partial reprogramming)
interventions.mechanism: HMG-box transcription factor involved in neural and pluripotent stem cell maintenance; partial epigenetic rejuvenation when expressed transiently
interventions.known_aging_effect: SOX2 alone can partially reset epigenetic aging in certain cell types; modest rejuvenation activity without full reprogramming
interventions.notes: Part of OSK and OSKM; individual factor activity is context-dependent and less studied than combinations

interventions.name: KLF4 alone (partial reprogramming)
interventions.mechanism: Krüppel-like factor 4; barrier to and facilitator of reprogramming; activates pluripotency network through chromatin remodeling
interventions.known_aging_effect: Individual Yamanaka factors can partially reset epigenetic age; KLF4 alone has modest rejuvenation effects in somatic cells
interventions.notes: KLF4 has tumor suppressor properties in some contexts, making it the lowest risk individual Yamanaka factor

interventions.name: Chemical reprogramming cocktail (7c)
interventions.mechanism: Seven small molecules (CHIR99021, Repsox, Forskolin, VPA, Tranylcypromine, TTNPB, DZNep) that replace Yamanaka factors for reprogramming via epigenetic modulation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Chemical reprogramming avoids viral integration and genetic modification; 7c cocktail achieves iPSC generation and partial reprogramming for rejuvenation
interventions.notes: Hou et al. 2013 — proof-of-principle; Tan et al. 2022 showed efficient chemical reprogramming. Transient application for rejuvenation under active development.

interventions.name: mRNA-based reprogramming (transient OSKM)
interventions.mechanism: Synthetic mRNA encoding Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, c-Myc delivered transiently; reprogramming factor expression is self-limiting (mRNA degrades) reducing oncogenic risk
interventions.known_aging_effect: Transient mRNA-OSKM expression resets epigenetic age without genomic integration; avoids sustained oncogene expression
interventions.notes: Warren et al. 2010 — original mRNA reprogramming; safety advantage is transient expression; delivery efficiency is a limitation

interventions.name: Beta-hydroxybutyrate (ketone body supplementation)
interventions.mechanism: Endogenous ketone body that acts as class I HDAC inhibitor; increases H3K9ac and H3K14ac; also activates HCAR2/GPR109A and signals through Nlrp3 inflammasome suppression
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in C. elegans; HDAC inhibitor activity modulates aging gene programs; improves cognitive function in aged mice
interventions.notes: Produced endogenously during fasting; exogenous supplementation via ketone esters or BHB salts; ketogenic diet achieves similar effects

interventions.name: Taurine supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Semi-essential amino acid declining with age; mitochondrial membrane stabilizer, calcium regulator, and osmolyte; reduces oxidative stress and mitochondrial dysfunction
interventions.known_aging_effect: Taurine declines >80% from youth to old age; supplementation extends lifespan in mice by ~12%; reduces hallmarks of aging across tissues
interventions.notes: Singh et al. 2023 Science — landmark paper on taurine decline as driver of aging

interventions.name: Glycine supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Non-essential amino acid acting as mTOR inhibitor at high doses; required for collagen synthesis; activates GCN2 amino acid sensing pathway
interventions.known_aging_effect: Glycine + N-acetyl cysteine (GlyNAC) supplementation reverses multiple aging hallmarks in humans; extends lifespan in Drosophila; corrects mitochondrial dysfunction
interventions.notes: Kumar et al. 2021 — GlyNAC human trial showing remarkable reversal of aging hallmarks in older adults

interventions.name: NAC (N-acetyl cysteine)
interventions.mechanism: Precursor to glutathione (GSH); increases intracellular cysteine availability for GSH synthesis; direct ROS scavenger and mucolytic agent
interventions.known_aging_effect: NAC alone extends lifespan modestly in some models; in combination with glycine (GlyNAC) powerfully reverses aging hallmarks in humans
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for acetaminophen overdose and mucolytic; GlyNAC combination is more effective than either alone

interventions.name: Urolithin A (mitophagy inducer)
interventions.mechanism: Gut microbiome-derived metabolite from ellagitannins (pomegranates, walnuts); activates mitophagy without mTOR inhibition; activates PINK1/Parkin pathway
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in C. elegans and improves muscle function in aged mice; human clinical trials showed improved mitochondrial biogenesis markers
interventions.notes: Ryu et al. 2016 Cell Metabolism landmark paper; individual variation in gut microbiome affects urolithin A production from diet

interventions.name: Trehalose (autophagy inducer)
interventions.mechanism: Non-reducing disaccharide; induces autophagy via mTOR-independent TFEB activation and by acting as chemical chaperone; also activates AMPK
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces protein aggregation; extends lifespan in C. elegans; reduces atherosclerosis in mice; protects against neurodegeneration
interventions.notes: Natural sugar with excellent safety profile; oral bioavailability limited by intestinal trehalase; IV administration more effective

interventions.name: Wnt inhibitor — PRI-724 (CBP/β-catenin)
interventions.mechanism: Inhibits β-catenin/CBP interaction; redirects Wnt signaling towards p300-mediated transcription; modulates stem cell renewal vs differentiation balance
interventions.known_aging_effect: Wnt hyperactivation contributes to tissue aging and fibrosis; PRI-724 reduces fibrosis and promotes tissue regeneration in aged mice
interventions.notes: Phase 1/2 clinical trials completed for cancer; aging applications under investigation

interventions.name: Notch inhibitor — gamma-secretase inhibitor (DBZ)
interventions.mechanism: Dibenzazepine (DBZ) inhibits gamma-secretase, blocking Notch intracellular domain release; reduces Notch target gene expression
interventions.known_aging_effect: Notch hyperactivation in aged muscle stem cells impairs regeneration; DBZ treatment restores youthful regenerative capacity
interventions.notes: Conboy lab showed Notch/TGF-β imbalance in aging; gamma-secretase inhibitors have GI toxicity in sustained use

interventions.name: Hedgehog agonist — SAG (Smoothened agonist)
interventions.mechanism: Smoothened agonist activates Hedgehog pathway, promoting Gli1/2 transcription and stem cell self-renewal programs
interventions.known_aging_effect: Hedgehog signaling declines with age in multiple tissues; SAG treatment restores neural stem cell activity in aged brains
interventions.notes: Constitutive Hedgehog activation causes basal cell carcinoma; pulsed treatment may be safer; context-dependent risk

interventions.name: TGF-β inhibitor — SB505124 (ALK4/5/7 inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: Selective ALK4/5/7 inhibitor blocking TGF-β and Activin receptor kinases; inhibits SMAD2/3 phosphorylation and downstream fibrosis/aging gene programs
interventions.known_aging_effect: TGF-β/Activin elevation inhibits satellite cell and NSC function in aging; systemic inhibition in aged mice restores muscle regeneration and neurogenesis
interventions.notes: Conboy et al. systemic blood factor work; TGF-β inhibition also carries wound healing and immune risks

interventions.name: PI3K inhibitor — copanlisib (pan-PI3K)
interventions.mechanism: Pan-class I PI3K inhibitor; blocks p110α/β/δ/γ isoforms; reduces AKT/mTOR signaling axis associated with aging and cancer
interventions.known_aging_effect: PI3K pathway reduction extends lifespan across model organisms; PI3K inhibition mimics aspects of reduced IIS signaling
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for follicular lymphoma; overlapping mechanism with rapamycin; combination may cause excessive immunosuppression

interventions.name: Growth hormone receptor antagonist — pegvisomant
interventions.mechanism: Pegylated GH receptor antagonist; blocks GH-mediated IGF-1 production; reduces somatotropic signaling axis
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduced GH/IGF-1 signaling extends lifespan in multiple models; GH receptor knockout mice (Laron syndrome) are long-lived
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for acromegaly; Laron syndrome patients have low cancer rates; GH reduction may compromise muscle and bone in elderly

interventions.name: IGF-1R antibody (ganitumab / AMG-479)
interventions.mechanism: Monoclonal antibody blocking IGF-1 and IGF-2 binding to IGF-1R; reduces AKT/PI3K/mTOR signaling
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduced IGF-1R signaling is one of the most conserved longevity pathways; partial reduction (heterozygous) extends lifespan in mice
interventions.notes: Haploinsufficiency of IGF1R extends human healthspan; complete blockade risks metabolic complications; antibody therapy is expensive

interventions.name: Oxytocin supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Neuropeptide acting via OXTR; activates muscle and neural stem cell proliferation; declines in circulation with age
interventions.known_aging_effect: Oxytocin declines with age; systemic administration improves muscle regeneration in aged mice; proposed circulating rejuvenation factor
interventions.notes: Conboy lab — oxytocin identified as circulating factor depleted in aging; well-established safety from obstetric use

interventions.name: Senolytics — Piperlongumine
interventions.mechanism: Natural alkaloid; induces selective apoptosis in senescent cells by increasing ROS to lethal levels; exploits higher basal oxidative stress in senescent cells
interventions.known_aging_effect: Clears senescent cells in vitro and in vivo; reduces senescence burden in aged tissues; synergizes with other senolytics
interventions.notes: Natural product derived from long pepper; selectivity for senescent vs normal cells demonstrated; clinical development early stage

interventions.name: Rapamycin analog — INK128 (mTOR kinase inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: ATP-competitive mTOR kinase inhibitor (TORKi); inhibits both mTORC1 and mTORC2 more completely than rapalogs; suppresses 4E-BP1 phosphorylation
interventions.known_aging_effect: More complete translation suppression than rapamycin; extends lifespan in C. elegans; reduces age-related inflammation
interventions.notes: Research compound; mTORC2 inhibition may impair AKT activity and have metabolic side effects

interventions.name: Acarbose (alpha-glucosidase inhibitor / CR mimetic)
interventions.mechanism: Inhibits intestinal alpha-glucosidases; reduces postprandial glucose spikes; activates AMPK, reduces mTOR signaling, mimics aspects of dietary restriction
interventions.known_aging_effect: ITP study: extends median lifespan ~22% in male mice and ~5% in females; reduces cancer incidence; FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes
interventions.notes: Harrison et al. NIA ITP — reproducible lifespan extension in mice; mechanism may involve microbiome changes and SCFA production

interventions.name: Canagliflozin (SGLT2 inhibitor / CR mimetic)
interventions.mechanism: Inhibits SGLT2 kidney glucose transporter; causes glucosuria, reducing blood glucose; activates AMPK and shifts metabolism towards fat oxidation
interventions.known_aging_effect: NIA ITP: extends male mouse lifespan ~14% (more potent in males); female effect modest; approved for type 2 diabetes and heart failure
interventions.notes: Strong evidence from ITP; sex-specific effects are notable; cardiovascular and renal protective effects in humans

interventions.name: 17α-estradiol (non-feminizing estrogen / lifespan extension)
interventions.mechanism: Non-feminizing estrogen isomer; activates ERα differently from 17β-estradiol; reduces lipid accumulation, inflammation, and promotes metabolic health
interventions.known_aging_effect: NIA ITP: extends median lifespan ~19% in males only; does not feminize male mice; reduces cancer incidence
interventions.notes: One of the most robust ITP hits specifically in males; mechanism distinct from 17β-estradiol; human studies not yet done

interventions.name: Captopril (ACE inhibitor / lifespan extension)
interventions.mechanism: ACE inhibitor; reduces angiotensin II and reduces ROS, inflammation, and cellular senescence in vascular tissue
interventions.known_aging_effect: Enalapril (ACE inhibitor) extends lifespan in NIA ITP; reduces cardiovascular aging; potential rejuvenation of aged kidney and vasculature
interventions.notes: FDA-approved antihypertensive; enalapril is the ITP-tested compound; class effect likely

interventions.name: Lithium supplementation (low dose)
interventions.mechanism: GSK3β inhibitor at therapeutic doses; activates autophagy via mTOR-independent pathway; reduces Wnt pathway hyperactivation; neuroprotective
interventions.known_aging_effect: Low-dose lithium extends lifespan in C. elegans, Drosophila, and rodents; epidemiological data links regional lithium in water to reduced mortality
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for bipolar disorder; narrow therapeutic window at high doses; longevity effects at sub-therapeutic doses

interventions.name: Nicotinamide (NAM) supplementation
interventions.mechanism: NAD+ precursor via salvage pathway; also directly inhibits sirtuins (product inhibitor) at high doses — dose is critical for net effect
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in C. elegans; improves kidney and brain function in aged mice; reduces DNA damage; used topically for skin aging
interventions.notes: At high doses, nicotinamide inhibits sirtuins — lower doses or timed dosing may avoid this; different from nicotinamide riboside (NR)

interventions.name: Senolytics — Venetoclax (BCL-2 inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: Highly selective BCL-2 inhibitor (BH3 mimetic); selectively kills BCL-2-dependent senescent cells; FDA-approved for CLL
interventions.known_aging_effect: Effective senolytic for BCL-2-high senescent cell populations; complementary to BCL-XL inhibitors (navitoclax) for full senescent cell clearance
interventions.notes: FDA-approved; BCL-2 vs BCL-XL dependence varies by senescent cell type; venetoclax + navitoclax combination may clear broader senescent populations

interventions.name: Quercetin alone (senolytic / antioxidant)
interventions.mechanism: Flavonoid inhibiting PI3K, tyrosine kinases, and BCL-2 family proteins in senescent cells; also activates autophagy and has antioxidant activity
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces senescent cell burden alone; weaker than D+Q combination but more accessible; epidemiological association with reduced mortality
interventions.notes: Component of the D+Q combination (INT-005); used alone in some aging studies; bioavailability improves with piperine co-administration

interventions.name: Luteolin (senolytic / anti-SASP flavonoid)
interventions.mechanism: Flavonoid with senolytic properties; inhibits PI3K/AKT survival signaling in senescent cells; reduces NF-κB-mediated SASP gene expression
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reduces senescent cell burden in aged mice; improves physical function; synergizes with dasatinib; cognitive and anti-inflammatory benefits in aged rodents
interventions.notes: Natural flavonoid from vegetables and herbs; lower potency than fisetin but better studied in combination protocols

interventions.name: Deferiprone (iron chelation)
interventions.mechanism: Iron chelator reducing age-associated iron accumulation; labile iron pool elevation drives Fenton chemistry and hydroxyl radical generation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Iron accumulates in aging tissues; deferiprone reduces iron burden, oxidative damage, and extends lifespan in model organisms
interventions.notes: FDA-approved for thalassemia; aging-relevant iron accumulation increasingly recognized as a driver of senescence and ferroptosis

interventions.name: Allantoin / HPOP supplementation
interventions.mechanism: Purine catabolism product; activates imidazoline receptors and AMPK; modulatory effects on mTOR/AMPK axis
interventions.known_aging_effect: Extends lifespan in C. elegans via mTOR-dependent mechanism; aging effects in mammals under investigation
interventions.notes: Natural metabolite found in many plants and animals; generally recognized as safe; mammalian longevity data limited

interventions.name: Dasatinib alone (senolytic tyrosine kinase inhibitor)
interventions.mechanism: BCR-ABL, c-Kit, PDGFR, and Src family kinase inhibitor; selectively kills senescent cells by targeting kinases in their aberrant survival networks
interventions.known_aging_effect: Effective senolytic alone; FDA-approved for CML; used in clinical aging trials as component of D+Q but also studied alone
interventions.notes: Component of the D+Q combination (INT-005); studied independently for senolytic activity; weekly pulsed dosing used in aging trials

interventions.name: Exendin-4 / Exenatide (GLP-1R agonist)
interventions.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist; stimulates insulin secretion, reduces glucagon, promotes satiety, and activates neuroprotective pathways via cAMP/PKA
interventions.known_aging_effect: GLP-1R agonists reduce obesity-associated aging; neuroprotective in Parkinson's models; reduces inflammation and cellular senescence
interventions.notes: FDA-approved class (exenatide, semaglutide, liraglutide); metabolic benefits in obese aged individuals; aging-specific mechanisms being characterized

interventions.name: Semaglutide (GLP-1R agonist / long-acting)
interventions.mechanism: Long-acting GLP-1R agonist; reduces weight, inflammation, and improves metabolic parameters; reduces NF-κB activation and SASP markers
interventions.known_aging_effect: SELECT trial: significant reduction in cardiovascular events in non-diabetic obese individuals; emerging evidence for reduced biological aging rate
interventions.notes: Rapidly gaining interest as a longevity drug; SELECT trial is one of the largest cardiovascular outcome trials; aging mechanism characterization ongoing

interventions.name: ISRIB (ISR inhibitor / proteostasis)
interventions.mechanism: Integrated Stress Response Inhibitor; stabilizes eIF2B, the guanine nucleotide exchange factor for eIF2α; reverses translational repression caused by ISR activation
interventions.known_aging_effect: Reverses age-related cognitive decline in aged mice; restores youthful translation and proteostasis; improves memory within hours of treatment
interventions.notes: Remarkable cognitive rejuvenation in aged mice; Villeda/Walter lab; drug-like properties with brain penetrance

interventions.name: Young plasma / heterochronic parabiosis factors
interventions.mechanism: Young blood plasma contains pro-rejuvenation factors (GDF11, GPLD1, etc.) and lacks pro-aging factors; systemic administration transfers these
interventions.known_aging_effect: Young plasma infusion improves cognition, muscle, and liver in aged mice; young plasma factors activate neurogenesis and tissue regeneration
interventions.notes: Mechanism is multi-factorial and debated; dilution of old plasma (apheresis) may be as effective as addition of young plasma; human trials ongoing (Alkahest)

interventions.name: GPLD1 supplementation (GPI-PLD, exercise factor)
interventions.mechanism: Glycosylphosphatidylinositol-specific phospholipase D1; liver-secreted factor increased by exercise; activates TET demethylase activity in the brain
interventions.known_aging_effect: GPLD1 is elevated by exercise and in young blood; injection improves cognition in aged mice; mediates some benefits of exercise on brain aging
interventions.notes: Horowitz et al. 2020 Science — liver-derived exercise factor mediating brain rejuvenation; high interest but translation challenges

interventions.name: Plasma dilution / therapeutic apheresis
interventions.mechanism: Dilutes age-elevated inhibitory factors in old blood (TGF-β1, CCL11/eotaxin, B2M) without requiring young plasma; replaces with albumin/saline
interventions.known_aging_effect: Plasma dilution alone (without young plasma) restores youthful gene expression in liver; improves muscle and brain function in aged mice
interventions.notes: Mehdipour et al. 2021 — reframes heterochronic parabiosis; clinical trials underway (Gordian Biotechnology)
